replies.msg: 就返鬼佬時間

先用Google Translate
再慢慢執番個formal?

翻譯得唔係太好
希望有人可以幫我執一執下

Today, the government of the People's Republic of China announced it would implement a new National Law in Hong Kong. This Law egregiously violates the Joint Sino-British Declaration and also greatly diminishes Hong Kong's status as an international finance center. As a Hong Kong citizen, I strongly urge the United Kingdom to exercise its responsibility to uphold the freedom and way of life of Hong Kong - as it agreed to in the Joint Sino-British Declaration - and respond to the Chinese government's appalling interference in Hong Kong's autonomy by terminating the Declaration and resuming sovereignty over Hong Kong. The Declaration clearly states that the Chinese socialist system will not be practiced in Hong Kong, and that Hong Kong's capitalist system and way of life will be maintained for 50 years until 2047.

Hong Kong citizens are extremely displeased with the Hong Kong SAR government's policies over the past year - especially surrounding the extradition bill and its subsequent handling of the issue. Unfortunately, the SAR government has not only refused to listen to our citizen's demands, but instead continues to use violence to suppress our demands. Examples of this violence include the police shooting and blinding a journalist covering the protests, lynching injured protestors in hospital, among countless others.

Hong Kong's societal issues should be resolved in accordance to Hong Kong's capitalistic, free principles. Instead, the Chinese Communist government wants to resolve this with the National Security Law, which is steeped with Chinese socialistic principles. We strongly believe that Hong Kong's human rights and freedoms will be severely eroded once the National Security Law is implemented, and that our judicial system will become non-independent and entirely subordinate to the Chinese Communist Party. The transparency of Hong Kong's financial system will also be substantially weakened.

If this law is implemented, Hong Kong will be governed by laws from two opposing systems: ones born from Hong Kong's capitalist system, and ones imposed upon us from the People's Republic of China's socialist system. This is prohibited under the Joint Sino-British Declaration.

I strongly urge the UK government to seriously deal with China's serious breaches of the Declaration.

quote.msg:

replies.msg:
我本身有諗過 啲單字識譯 但文法一定錯到hihi
利：中學水平

啱啱簡單send咗段，大家認為有用嘅可以keep


To whom it may concern,

I concern what is happening in Hongkong, a former British colony before 1997. China is violating the Basic Law because China decided to pass the controversial security law by themselves. This overrides the normal legislative structure and breaches the promise guaranteed to the Hongkong people under the Sino-British Joint Declaration.

The US President Donald Trump expressed his biggest concern to warn China and emphasises that further strong actions would be implemented if Beijing does not step back.

Here, I urge the UK Government to think deeply on how to save the Hongkong people when the guaranteed freedom of speech, judiciary system, democracy etc are corroded by China.

https://www.theguardian.com/world/2020/may/21/china-proposes-controversial-national-security-law-for-hong-kong

Thanks and Regards,

quote.msg: Today -> 21th May

Unfortunately, the SAR government has not only refused to listen to our citizen's demands, but instead continues to use violence to suppress our demands. Examples of this violence include the police shooting and blinding a journalist covering the protests, torturing injured protestors in hospital, among countless others.

Hong Kong's societal issues should be resolved in accordance to Hong Kong's capitalistic, free principles. Instead, the Chinese Communist government wants to resolve this with the National Security Law, which is based on Chinese socialistic principles. We strongly believe that Hong Kong's human rights and freedoms will be severely eroded once the National Security Law is implemented, and that our judicial system will become non-independent and entirely subordinate to the Chinese Communist Party. The transparency of Hong Kong's financial system will also be substantially weakened.

If this law is implemented, Hong Kong will be governed by laws from two opposing systems: ones born from Hong Kong's capitalist system, and ones imposed upon us from the People's Republic of China's socialist system. This is prohibited under the Joint Sino-British Declaration.

I strongly urge the UK government to seriously deal with China's serious breaches of the Declaration.

replies.msg:
firm version, 可以用黎做懶人包， 麻煩哂

Today, the government of the People's Republic of China announced it would implement the new National Security Law in Hong Kong.

This Law egregiously violates the Joint Sino-British Declaration and also greatly diminishes Hong Kong's status as an international finance center. As a Hong Kong citizen, I strongly urge the United Kingdom to exercise its responsibility to uphold the freedom and way of life of Hong Kong, as it agreed to do in the Joint Sino-British Declaration. The Declaration clearly states that the Chinese socialist system will not be practiced in Hong Kong, and that Hong Kong's capitalist system and way of life will be maintained for 50 years until 2047.

I strongly urge the UK to respond to the Chinese government's appalling interference in Hong Kong's autonomy by terminating the Declaration and resuming sovereignty over Hong Kong.

Hong Kong citizens are extremely displeased with the Hong Kong SAR government's policies over the past year - especially surrounding the extradition bill and its subsequent handling of the issue. Unfortunately, the SAR government has not only refused to listen to our citizen's demands, but instead continues to use violence to suppress our demands. Examples of this violence include the police shooting and blinding a journalist covering the protests, torturing injured protestors in hospital, among countless others.

Hong Kong's societal issues should be resolved in accordance to Hong Kong's capitalistic, free principles. Instead, the Chinese Communist government wants to resolve this with the National Security Law, which is based on Chinese socialistic principles. We strongly believe that Hong Kong's human rights and freedoms will be severely eroded once the National Security Law is implemented, and that our judicial system will become non-independent and entirely subordinate to the Chinese Communist Party. The transparency of Hong Kong's financial system will also be substantially weakened.

If this law is implemented, Hong Kong will be governed by laws from two opposing systems: ones born from Hong Kong's capitalist system, and ones imposed upon us from the People's Republic of China's socialist system. This is in direct contravention to the Joint Sino-British Declaration.

I strongly urge the UK government to seriously deal with China's serious breaches of the Declaration.
